mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when RSAFS is trending upward over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that raises real discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment combined with restrictive Fed policy indicates an overheating labor market that forces additional monetary tightening; the joint effect of UNRATE below 5% and FEDFUNDS above 4% produces negative equity returns that are not captured by either series in isolation.
failure_condition: A two-factor interaction model fails to outperform a single-factor FEDFUNDS model by more than 0.02 in Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect accelerating construction activity and household formation, which drive durable goods demand and credit expansion to produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next week during post-shock recoveries when pent-up housing demand is being released.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of weeks when HOUST is rising during the post-shock recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth, as measured by GDPC1, captures the aggregate output expansion that drives economy-wide corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 changes and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production below minus 0.5% signal acute supply-side contraction and inventory destocking, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as earnings expectations are cut.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances when INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth increases household income and consumption, reducing recession risk and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through higher aggregate demand and earnings.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in more than 45% of months with positive month-over-month PAYEMS growth over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation above 2% combined with a federal funds rate above 4% forces the central bank to maintain restrictive policy despite growth risks; the interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter that exceed the standalone effects of either variable.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a bivariate regression adds less than 0.01 incremental R-squared relative to single-factor models over a 2-year rolling window.
